task_menu_sidebar_description: Toggle between sidebar and floating menu for tasks.
bypass_page_visibility_description: Show all system pages regardless of task level restrictions.
tasks_description: Manage and create tasks for your information system.
teacher_page_description: Welcome to the teacher area. From here you can manage tasks and use the designer.
download_system_description: Downloads the entire system, including the current state of the database, all components and tasks
task_designer_description: Design and build tasks for your information systems.
homepage_welcome_message: Welcome to the error-finding application for information systems
manage_your_systems_description: This is the teacher version of the application, which allows you to add tasks to information systems. If you would like to create your own system, it is possible—please refer to the guide, specifically the section: System Creation.
all_tasks_completed: All tasks have been successfully completed
student_welcome_modal_description: Complete tasks and earn points. The task panel shows assignments, progress, and evaluation of your answers.
student_completed_modal_description: Great work, you have completed all available tasks in this information system.
dark_mode_description: Switches the app appearance between light and dark mode.
refresh_system_modal_description: Choose which part of the system you want to refresh.
refresh_database_modal_option_descriptio: Resets database data to its original state.
refresh_components_modal_option_descript: Restores edited components to their default state. Database data will be kept.
refresh_all_modal_option_description: Returns the system to its default state. Component changes, database data, and task progress will be lost.
intro: The application provides various types of tasks. The following section explains how such tasks work and how they are created.
solution_1_desc: The goal of this task is for the student to find the component that is not functioning correctly. The student marks such a component and submits it.
solution_2_desc: The goal of this task is for the student to repair the component that is not functioning correctly. The student makes the necessary adjustments and submits the repaired component.
solution_3_desc: The goal of this task is for the student to answer a question related to the component. The student selects one of the answers and submits it.
creation_intro: All task creation currently takes place purely through the config.json file, which can be found at
creation_1_desc: Each component has some parts that can be modified through the config.json file. For example, it is possible to edit the SQL query or HTML template.
creation_2_desc: For each component, there is a dual matching .error component, see ./components/. In the config.json file, it is possible to specify that the faulty component should be used.
how_to_title: How to create tasks using the config.json file
sessions_description: Overview of all sessions in the system
no_sessions_description: There are currently no sessions in the system. You can create a new session.
name: Name
no_participants_enrolled: No participants are enrolled in this session
no_supervisors_assigned: No supervisors are assigned to this session
no_meals_description: There are currently no meals scheduled in the system.
search_placeholder: Search by meal name, when served, allergens, or date...
change_version_modal_description: Switch between the teacher and student version for this system. Students cannot switch between versions.
html_block_info_description: The component HTML template. It defines the visible elements that students or teachers interact with.
css_block_info_description: The component CSS styles. They control the visual appearance of the HTML elements in this component.
js_block_info_description: The component JavaScript. Use it for local calculations, state, and values the component can display.
sql_block_info_description: SQL queries that run when the component loads. Their results become available as SQL variables.
js_click_block_info_description: JavaScript that runs after clicking the component. Use it for custom click interactions.
sql_click_block_info_description: SQL queries that run after clicking the component. Useful for data changes or click-triggered actions.
click_actions_info_description: Click actions run when the user clicks the component. They can be JavaScript logic or SQL queries.
general_variables_info_description: General variables are available to the component independently of SQL queries, such as values provided by the system or task.
sql_variables_info_description: SQL variables come from this component's SQL query results and can be used in JavaScript or when displaying data.
js_variables_info_description: JS variables come from the component JavaScript and can be used when composing the component content.
no_participants_description: There are currently no participants in the system.
no_supervisors_description: There are currently no supervisors in the system.
no_meal_plan_description: There is currently no meal plan data in the system.
select_participant_and_meal: Please select both a participant and a meal
select_supervisor_and_meal: Please select both a supervisor and a meal
select_participant_meal_and_date: Please select a participant, meal, and date
select_supervisor_meal_and_date: Please select a supervisor, meal, and date
no_valid_system_found: No valid system found in the uploaded file
information_systems: Welcome to the Information Systems Learning App
no_systems_description: Get started by adding your first information system
system_with_same_id_exists: A system with the same ID already exists
clear_systems_warning: This action will permanently remove all systems. This cannot be undone.
settings_description: Customize your application preferences and shortcuts
task_activity_repair_info: The student must fix the component code
task_activity_select_info: The student must select the correct components
task_activity_select_options_info: The student must select the correct answer from the options
task_substitute_notice: After completing the activity, faulty components will be automatically replaced with the correct ones.
task_page_unavailable_description: The page is not available during this task.
task_level_locked_description: Complete all tasks from the current level.
task_level_visible_pages_mismatch_descri: Tasks in the same level should have the same visible pages.
task_round_info: Level in which the task is completed
task_level_info: Level in which the task is completed
task_level_count_info: How many levels this information system has
task_level_no_tasks: There are no tasks in this level.
task_points_reward_info: How many points the student receives for completing the task
task_export_component_desc: Click to copy the JSON definition of this component to the clipboard for backup or transfer.
task_edit_component_desc: Opens the editor to modify the code (HTML, SQL, JS) of the selected component.
task_remove_component_desc: Removes this component from the list of error components for this task.
task_fail_penalty_info: How many points the student loses for failing the task
task_can_execute_query_description: The student will see the SQL editor on the database page for this task.
task_selected_components_info: Select components in the system by clicking their name in the blue badge, then edit the component code and create some mistakes in it. When you return to the system, you will see these error components.
task_substitute_checkbox: Replace error components with originals after activity completes
task_substitute_after_activity_hint: For example, after the student answers the question correctly, the system will use the correct component instead of the error one.
task_substitute_after_activity_repair_di: For component repair, this option is disabled because the student should fix the mistake directly in the component code.
task_feedback_info: A message shown to the student after the task is completed, once both the activity and task finish are done.
task_options_info: A green check mark marks a correct answer, while an X marks an incorrect answer. The student must correctly select all correct answers to solve it.
task_modal_description: Create and edit tasks for the selected system.
task_remove_task_description: Deletes this task from the system.
task_import_description: Paste a JSON task serialization below or upload a file.
task_import_error_json: Invalid JSON. Please check the pasted content.
task_import_all_description: Paste a JSON array of tasks or upload a file. Existing tasks will be replaced.
task_import_all_error_array: Expected a JSON array of tasks.
task_preview_editor_desc: You are currently viewing the task editor. Click to see how the student will see the task during its solution.
task_preview_student_desc: You are currently viewing the task as the student will see it. Click to return to the editor.
task_finish_immediate_default_descriptio: Task will be completed immediately after the activity is finished
refresh_database_popover_description: Returns all tables to their original state.
database_query_disabled_title: SQL queries are not enabled for this task
database_query_disabled_description: The teacher can enable the SQL editor in the task settings in the designer.
no_suggestions_available: No suggestions are available at this time.
system_already_exists: A system with this ID already exists
task_activity_not_completed_description: You must successfully complete the task's activity first before you can evaluate and finish the task.
